title: Narcissus luteolentus
intro: Narcissus luteolentus, also known as the yellow narcissus, is a perennial bulbous plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, contributing to its use in treating various health issues such as respiratory infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Narcissus luteolentus include alkaloids, such as lycorine and narciclasine, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. Narcissus luteolentus is often used in herbal preparations, including tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which are traditionally employed to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and as a natural antiseptic.
taxonomy: Narcissus luteolentus is classified in the family Amaryllidaceae and is a species of flowering plant. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other species. The genus Narcissus is further classified into sections, with Narcissus luteolentus belonging to the section Pseudonarcissus. This section is characterized by its unique flower shape and color. Additionally, the species is further subdivided into various cultivars and varieties.
benefits: Narcissus luteolentus is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential health benefits.
